expectations.text: Uses curl install script with specific version pin (INSTALL_K3S_VERSION)
expectations.evidence: Shows `INSTALL_K3S_VERSION=v1.32.3+k3s1` in the curl install command.

expectations.text: Includes --tls-san with the public IP
expectations.evidence: Shows `--tls-san=203.0.113.50` in the install flags.

expectations.text: Includes --disable=traefik
expectations.evidence: Shows `--disable=traefik` with note "lets you install ingress-nginx later for more control".

expectations.text: Includes --cluster-init for future HA capability
expectations.evidence: Shows `--cluster-init` with note "uses embedded etcd (enables future HA expansion)".

expectations.text: Copies kubeconfig to local machine and updates server address from 127.0.0.1 to public IP
expectations.evidence: Shows `ssh root@203.0.113.50 cat /etc/rancher/k3s/k3s.yaml > ~/clusters/hetzner.yaml` then editing server from `127.0.0.1` to `203.0.113.50`.

expectations.text: Verifies with kubectl get nodes
expectations.evidence: Shows `kubectl get nodes` as the final verification step.

expectations.text: Uses curl install script with specific version pin (INSTALL_K3S_VERSION)
expectations.evidence: Shows `curl -sfL https://get.k3s.io | sh -` with no INSTALL_K3S_VERSION pin.

expectations.text: Includes --tls-san with the public IP
expectations.evidence: No --tls-san flag in the install command. The API server certificate won't include the public IP.

expectations.text: Includes --disable=traefik
expectations.evidence: No --disable=traefik. Notes "k3s includes Traefik (ingress)...out of the box" without suggesting disabling it.

expectations.text: Includes --cluster-init for future HA capability
expectations.evidence: No --cluster-init flag. Single-node SQLite install with no HA path.

expectations.text: Copies kubeconfig to local machine and updates server address from 127.0.0.1 to public IP
expectations.evidence: Shows `scp root@203.0.113.50:/etc/rancher/k3s/k3s.yaml ~/.kube/config` and `sed -i '' 's/127.0.0.1/203.0.113.50/'`.

expectations.text: Verifies with kubectl get nodes
expectations.evidence: Shows `kubectl get nodes` as the final verification step.

expectations.text: Retrieves join token from the correct location
expectations.evidence: Shows `cat /var/lib/rancher/k3s/server/token` on node0.

expectations.text: Joins server node with server subcommand and --server flag pointing to node0
expectations.evidence: Shows `sh -s - server --server https://NODE0_IP:6443 --token YOUR_TOKEN`.

expectations.text: Matches original install flags (--disable=traefik, --cluster-domain if set)
expectations.evidence: Includes `--disable=traefik` in the node1 server join command.

expectations.text: Joins agent node with agent subcommand (distinct from server join)
expectations.evidence: Shows `sh -s - agent --server https://NODE0_IP:6443 --token YOUR_TOKEN` for node2.

expectations.text: Pins INSTALL_K3S_VERSION to match existing cluster
expectations.evidence: Shows `INSTALL_K3S_VERSION=v1.32.3+k3s1` for both node1 and node2 installs.

expectations.text: Confirms mixed architecture (ARM64 + x86-64) works without special config
expectations.evidence: "ARM64 works because k3s is multi-arch; the same version handles both architectures."

expectations.text: Retrieves join token from the correct location
expectations.evidence: Shows `cat /var/lib/rancher/k3s/server/token` on node0.

expectations.text: Joins server node with server subcommand and --server flag pointing to node0
expectations.evidence: Shows `sh -s - server --server https://<node0-ip>:6443` for node1.

expectations.text: Matches original install flags (--disable=traefik, --cluster-domain if set)
expectations.evidence: No --disable=traefik in the node1 join command. Original install flags not matched.

expectations.text: Joins agent node with agent subcommand (distinct from server join)
expectations.evidence: Shows `K3S_URL=... K3S_TOKEN=... sh -` for node2 — the env-var shorthand that invokes agent mode.

expectations.text: Pins INSTALL_K3S_VERSION to match existing cluster
expectations.evidence: No INSTALL_K3S_VERSION pin in any of the join commands.

expectations.text: Confirms mixed architecture (ARM64 + x86-64) works without special config
expectations.evidence: "k3s supports ARM64 natively — the install script detects architecture and downloads the correct binary."

expectations.text: Shows kubectl get for pods, deployments, services
expectations.evidence: Shows `kubectl get nodes`, `kubectl get pods -A`, `kubectl get pods -n production` and deployment/service commands.

expectations.text: Shows kubectl describe for debugging
expectations.evidence: Shows `kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n <ns>` with note "Events section at the bottom shows why something failed".

expectations.text: Shows kubectl logs with -f and/or --previous
expectations.evidence: Shows `kubectl logs <pod-name> -n <namespace> -f` and `kubectl logs --previous <pod-name>`.

expectations.text: Shows kubectl exec for interactive shell access
expectations.evidence: Shows `kubectl exec -it <pod-name> -n <ns> -- /bin/sh`.

expectations.text: Shows kubectl port-forward
expectations.evidence: Shows both `kubectl port-forward pod/<name> 8080:80` and `kubectl port-forward svc/<name> 8080:80`.

expectations.text: Explains namespace flag (-n / --all-namespaces)
expectations.evidence: "Always specify -n <namespace> or use -A for all. Without it, kubectl uses the default namespace."

expectations.evidence: Shows `kubectl logs <pod-name> -n <ns> --previous` and `kubectl logs <pod-name> -n <ns> -f`.
